deductions.reason: The tutor never explains why it is guiding rather than giving answers directly—no rationale is offered for the Socratic approach (e.g., that active reasoning builds retention or long-term research skills). The tutoring constraints are consistently applied but never justified to the student.

deductions.evidence_turns: Transcript 1 Turn 10, Transcript 2 Turn 10, Transcript 3 Turn 10
deductions.reason: In all three transcripts the student reaches a solid, improved understanding by the final turns, but the tutor never prompts reflection on what changed in the student's thinking or why the careful approach worked. The tutor simply moves forward to the next task without reinforcing the method choices the student made.

grade.overview: Across all three transcripts the tutor performs very well. It consistently uses Socratic questioning, never gives submission-ready answers, builds on student progress, maintains appropriate tone, and keeps responses concise and focused. The primary weaknesses are the absence of any explicit rationale for the tutoring approach (1.3.A.a) and the failure to prompt process reflection after the student reaches improved understanding (1.3.C.a). No other rubric criteria show clear evidence of deduction.
grade.judge_reasoning: 1.1: The tutor never provides direct answers, finished reasoning, or near-submission-ready content across all 30 turns. All key ideas are student-generated through guided questioning. No deductions apply. 1.2: The tutor consistently builds on correct student responses (e.g., affirming the DKI Jakarta boundary choice before advancing) and diagnoses confusion before responding. No deductions apply. 1.3: Two deductions are warranted. First, the tutor never explains why it is guiding rather than telling—no rationale for the Socratic method is offered in any transcript (1.3.A.a, -2). Second, in all three transcripts the student arrives at a well-reasoned position by the end, but the tutor simply issues the next task without asking the student to reflect on what changed in their thinking (1.3.C.a, -2). 2.1 and 2.2: No repetition without progress, no off-topic drift, and no misunderstood assignment framing goes uncorrected. 3.1 and 3.2: Responses are appropriately concise, tone is supportive throughout, and no grading language is used.
